word_list.word: a carper can cavil at anything
word_list.phonetic_text: ə kɑr.pɜr kæn kæ.vəl æt ɛ.ni.θɪng
word_list.phonetic_am_text: ē kaar.pēr kān kā.vēl āt e.ni.thing
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ kˈɑːɹpɚ kæn kˈævəl æt ˈɛnɪθˌɪŋ

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person who is overly critical or fault-finding can find fault with anything, regardless of its merit or quality

word_list.word: a little pot is soon hot
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈɪɾəl pˈɑːt ɪz sˈuːn hˈɑːt

senses.definition: used to imply that those who lack emotional maturity or self-control may be quick to overreact to minor issues

word_list.word: sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof
word_list.pronunciation: səfˈɪʃənt ˌʌntʊ ðə dˈeɪ ɪz ðɪ ˈiːvəl ðɛɹˈɑːv

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is best to deal with the challenges and difficulties of each day as they come, without adding unnecessary worry about what may or may not happen in the future

word_list.pronunciation: ðɪ ɐ skwˈiːki wˈiːl ˈɔːlweɪz ɡˈɛts ðə ɡɹˈiːs ˈɔɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that those who speak up or make their needs known are more likely to be noticed or helped than those who remain silent or passive

word_list.word: as water reflects the face, so one's life reflects the heart
word_list.pronunciation: æz wˈɔːɾɚ ɹɪflˈɛkts ðə fˈeɪs sˌoʊ wˈʌnz lˈaɪf ɹɪflˈɛkts ðə hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person's character and true nature are revealed through the way they live their life

word_list.word: life is ten percent what happens to you and ninety percent how you (respond|react) to it
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz tˈɛn pɚsˈɛnt wˌʌt hˈæpənz tə juː ænd nˈaɪnti pɚsˈɛnt hˌaʊ juː ɹɪspˈɑːnd ɔːɹ ɹɪˈækt tʊ ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's attitude, mindset, and actions have a greater impact on their life than external circumstances

word_list.word: the same fire that melts the butter, hardens the egg
word_list.pronunciation: ðə sˈeɪm fˈaɪɚ ðæt mˈɛlts ðə bˈʌɾɚ hˈɑːɹdənz ðɪ ˈɛɡ

senses.definition: used to imply that people respond differently to the same external circumstances based on their individual traits, and that external circumstances alone do not determine a person's outcome

word_list.word: take each day as it comes
word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk ˈiːtʃ dˈeɪ æz ɪt kˈʌmz

senses.definition: used to advise to focus on the present, deal with challenges as they arise, and not to worry too much about the future

word_list.word: take not a musket to kill a butterfly
word_list.phonetic_text: teɪk nɑt ə məs.kət tu: kɪl ə bʌ.tər.flaɪ
word_list.phonetic_am_text: teik naat ē mēs.kēt too kil ē ba.tēr.flai
word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk nˌɑːɾə mˈʌskɪt tə kˈɪl ɐ bˈʌɾɚflˌaɪ

senses.definition: used to imply that it is important to use the appropriate tool or approach to accomplish a task, and not to use excessive force or resources

word_list.word: there is more than one way to skin a cat
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz mˈoːɹ ðɐn wˈʌn wˈeɪ tə skˈɪn ɐ kˈæt

senses.definition: used to suggest that there are multiple paths to success, and that it is important to be open-minded and flexible in one's approach to problem-solving or achieving goals

examples.x: Sarah's boss reminded her that there is more than one way to skin a cat when she was stuck on a project, and encouraged her to think outside the box and explore creative solutions.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡɹˈeɪt mˈʌtʃ kɹˈaɪ ænd lˈɪɾəl wˈʊl

senses.definition: used to refer to the act of making a lot of fuss or noise over something that is insignificant or unimportant

word_list.word: there are more ways to the wood than one
word_list.phonetic_text: ðɛr ɑr mɔr weɪz tu: ðə wʊd ðæn n
word_list.phonetic_am_text: dher aar mawr veiz too dhē vood dhān n
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹˌɑːɹ mˈoːɹ wˈeɪz tə ðə wˈʊd ðɐn wˌʌn

senses.definition: used to imply that there are multiple ways to approach a situation or solve a problem, and encourages individuals to be open-minded and flexible in finding a solution
